title: AWS Cloud Practitioner Full Exam Prep
description: Comprehensive AWS Cloud Practitioner (CLF-C02) practice deck containing 120 questions covering all exam domains.

questions.question: Which AWS pricing idea means customers pay only for what they use?
questions.explanation: AWS commonly charges based on actual usage.

questions.question: Which AWS tool helps estimate costs before deploying services?
questions.explanation: The AWS Pricing Calculator helps estimate service costs.

questions.question: Which AWS tool helps visualize historical costs and usage trends?
questions.explanation: Cost Explorer shows historical usage and spending patterns.

questions.question: Which AWS service helps set spending thresholds and receive alerts?
questions.explanation: AWS Budgets lets customers create cost and usage thresholds with notifications.

questions.question: Which purchasing model is usually most flexible for short-term, unpredictable EC2 workloads?
questions.explanation: On-Demand pricing is flexible when workloads are unpredictable.

questions.question: Which pricing approach can lower cost for predictable long-running usage?
questions.explanation: Longer commitments can reduce price for steady workloads.

questions.question: Which EC2 option can be cheapest for interruptible workloads?
questions.explanation: Spot Instances offer major discounts for flexible, interruptible workloads.

questions.question: What is the AWS Free Tier mainly designed to do?
answers: Let customers explore services at low or no cost within limits
questions.explanation: The Free Tier helps users try services within defined limits.

questions.question: Which support plan includes basic account and billing support at no additional cost?
questions.explanation: Basic Support includes billing and account assistance.

questions.question: Which support plan is intended for workloads requiring 24/7 access to cloud support engineers?
questions.explanation: Business Support includes 24/7 access to cloud support engineers.

questions.question: Which feature helps organizations combine billing across multiple AWS accounts?
questions.explanation: Consolidated billing combines charges across multiple linked AWS accounts.

questions.question: What is a common reason to use AWS Organizations with multiple accounts?
questions.explanation: AWS Organizations helps manage multiple accounts centrally.

questions.question: Which pricing factor often affects AWS cost besides compute time and storage size?
questions.explanation: Data transfer charges can materially affect total AWS cost.

questions.question: Which practice best helps avoid paying for idle cloud resources?
questions.explanation: Reviewing usage and rightsizing helps remove waste.

questions.question: Which metadata practice helps allocate AWS costs to teams or projects?
questions.explanation: Tags help group and report spending by business category.

questions.question: Why are cost allocation tags useful?
answers: They improve cost visibility across business units
questions.explanation: Cost allocation tags support chargeback and cost analysis.

questions.question: Which AWS report helps organizations analyze detailed usage and cost data?
questions.explanation: The Cost and Usage Report provides detailed billing and usage data.

questions.question: Which support resource provides architectural guidance and operational recommendations?
questions.explanation: AWS support plans and tools can provide guidance beyond break-fix issues.

questions.question: Why do companies often separate workloads into multiple accounts for billing and governance?
answers: It improves visibility, control, and ownership
It removes all need for IAM
It guarantees lower pricing on every service
questions.explanation: Multiple accounts improve financial visibility and governance boundaries.

questions.question: Which action best helps forecast future AWS spend?
answers: Review historical trends in Cost Explorer and planned changes
questions.explanation: Forecasting is strongest when historical trends are combined with upcoming usage plans.

questions.question: Which storage choice can lower cost for rarely accessed archive data?
answers: Move data to an archival storage class
Keep everything on the highest-cost class
Store all archives on EC2 root disks
questions.explanation: Cheaper archival storage classes reduce cost for infrequently accessed data.

questions.question: Which support plan feature is most relevant to production-critical cloud operations?
questions.explanation: Production workloads often need faster response and always-available support engineers.

questions.question: Which action most directly reduces surprise cloud bills?
answers: Budgets, alerts, and regular cost reviews
Using the root user more often
questions.explanation: Budgets and regular review improve early detection of unusual cost growth.

questions.question: Why do Savings Plans usually require confidence in future usage?
answers: They exchange commitment for lower prices
questions.explanation: Savings Plans lower cost in exchange for a usage commitment.

questions.question: Which cost practice best supports accountability in shared cloud environments?
questions.explanation: Ownership and tagging make cloud cost accountability easier.

questions.question: Which factor should be considered when comparing On-Demand and Spot pricing?
answers: Whether the workload can tolerate interruption
Whether the instance uses Linux only
questions.explanation: Spot is attractive only when interruptions are acceptable.

questions.question: What is the main value of setting budget alerts before month end?
answers: They provide early warning to take corrective action
questions.explanation: Early budget alerts give teams time to respond before overspending grows.

questions.question: Which practice best supports cost optimization over time?
answers: Continuous review of usage, architecture, and pricing choices
One-time review at initial launch only
questions.explanation: Cost optimization is ongoing because workloads and pricing evolve.

questions.question: Why is understanding data transfer pricing important in AWS?
answers: It can significantly affect total cost
It only matters for root users
It is included in every free tier
questions.explanation: Data transfer charges can be a meaningful part of the bill.

questions.question: Which practice most directly helps decide whether a support plan upgrade is worthwhile?
answers: Review operational criticality and response needs
questions.explanation: Support choice should align with workload criticality and response expectations.

questions.question: What is the main AWS advantage of elasticity?
questions.explanation: Elasticity lets capacity grow or shrink with demand.

questions.question: Which cloud model provides services to many customers over the internet?
questions.explanation: A public cloud serves many customers from provider-managed infrastructure.

questions.question: Which cost model is most associated with AWS?
questions.explanation: AWS pricing is primarily based on actual usage.

questions.question: Which AWS feature helps reduce time spent provisioning servers manually?
questions.explanation: Cloud resources can be provisioned quickly by customers when needed.

questions.question: Which term describes spreading workloads across multiple systems to improve uptime?
questions.explanation: High availability reduces the impact of individual component failures.

questions.question: What is a primary reason companies move from CapEx to OpEx with AWS?
answers: They pay for services as they use them
They must buy more physical servers
questions.explanation: AWS shifts much spending from upfront purchases to operational usage costs.

questions.question: Which AWS infrastructure concept provides isolated locations inside a Region?
questions.explanation: Availability Zones are separate facilities within a Region.

questions.question: Which AWS principle helps match resources to unpredictable traffic?
questions.explanation: Scalability allows workloads to handle changing traffic patterns.

questions.question: What is the AWS shared responsibility model about?
answers: Dividing security duties between AWS and the customer
Running workloads only in private clouds
questions.explanation: AWS secures the cloud, while customers secure what they run in it.

questions.question: Which scenario best fits the need for a second AWS Region?
questions.explanation: A second Region provides broader geographic resilience.

questions.question: Which cloud benefit helps teams experiment without long hardware procurement cycles?
questions.explanation: Cloud agility reduces time between idea and deployment.

questions.question: Which statement best describes fault tolerance?
answers: A system continues operating despite component failures
A system always uses one large server
questions.explanation: Fault tolerance is the ability to keep working even when some components fail.

questions.question: Why do customers benefit from AWS global infrastructure?
answers: They can place workloads closer to users and recovery sites
They must use one Region only
questions.explanation: AWS global infrastructure supports low latency and resilience choices.

questions.question: What does scalability describe?
answers: A system's ability to handle more or less load
questions.explanation: Scalability is the ability to adjust to workload changes.

questions.question: Which type of scaling adds more instances instead of making one instance larger?
questions.explanation: Horizontal scaling adds more resources side by side.

questions.question: Which type of scaling increases CPU or memory on one instance?
questions.explanation: Vertical scaling increases the size of a single resource.

questions.question: What does business agility in cloud computing usually mean?
answers: Faster response to new requirements and opportunities
questions.explanation: Cloud services help teams move faster when requirements change.

questions.question: Which description best fits infrastructure as code?
answers: Using spreadsheets as the only control plane
questions.explanation: Infrastructure as code treats infrastructure setup as managed definitions.

questions.question: Why is right-sizing important in AWS?
answers: It helps align resource size with workload needs
questions.explanation: Right-sizing reduces waste and improves efficiency.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of managed services from a cloud concepts perspective?
questions.explanation: Managed services let teams focus more on business value than routine operations.

questions.question: Which design improves resilience inside one Region for a web app?
questions.explanation: Multiple AZs reduce the impact of a single-facility failure.

questions.question: What is the key benefit of using automation in cloud operations?
questions.explanation: Automation improves speed, consistency, and repeatability.

questions.question: Which statement best describes cloud economics?
answers: Resources can be aligned more closely to actual demand
Only one support plan is allowed
questions.explanation: Cloud economics improve when capacity is matched to real usage.

questions.question: Why is multi-Region architecture more complex than multi-AZ architecture?
answers: It spans geographically separate Regions with more coordination
questions.explanation: Multi-Region adds extra design and operational complexity across Regions.

questions.question: Which approach best supports resilience for stateful cloud workloads?
answers: Designing for backup, replication, and recovery
questions.explanation: Stateful workloads need explicit recovery and resilience planning.

questions.question: Which model best fits workloads that run only at certain times and then shut down?
questions.explanation: Cloud consumption pricing is well suited to intermittent workloads.

questions.question: What is the main value of elasticity during seasonal traffic spikes?
answers: It reduces the need to size for peak all year
It prevents outages in every scenario
questions.explanation: Elasticity helps avoid paying for peak capacity during quiet periods.

questions.question: Which cloud concept is most closely tied to launching resources without waiting for procurement?
questions.explanation: On-demand provisioning speeds up access to needed resources.

questions.question: Why do many companies begin cloud adoption with variable or bursty workloads?
answers: These workloads benefit quickly from elastic capacity
questions.explanation: Variable workloads often show immediate value from cloud elasticity.

questions.question: Which AWS concept most directly supports global customer experience improvement?
answers: Deploying in Regions closer to users
questions.explanation: Deploying closer to users can lower latency and improve experience.

questions.question: Which AWS service provides virtual servers?
questions.explanation: Amazon EC2 provides resizable virtual machine instances.

questions.question: Which AWS service is primarily used for object storage?
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 is AWS object storage.

questions.question: Which AWS service is used for a managed relational database?
questions.explanation: Amazon RDS is a managed relational database service.

questions.question: Which AWS service runs code without managing servers?
questions.explanation: AWS Lambda is AWS serverless compute.

questions.question: Which AWS service improves global delivery of cached content?
questions.explanation: CloudFront uses edge locations to deliver content with lower latency.

questions.question: Which AWS service creates an isolated virtual network?
questions.explanation: Amazon VPC provides a logically isolated virtual network.

questions.question: Which storage service provides block storage for EC2 instances?
questions.explanation: Amazon EBS provides persistent block storage volumes for EC2.

questions.question: Which storage service offers shared file storage for Linux workloads?
questions.explanation: Amazon EFS provides scalable shared file storage.

questions.question: Which DNS service routes internet traffic to AWS resources?
questions.explanation: Amazon Route 53 is AWS's scalable DNS service.

questions.question: Which service helps distribute traffic across multiple targets?
questions.explanation: Elastic Load Balancing spreads traffic across multiple healthy targets.

questions.question: Which AWS service provides NoSQL key-value storage?
questions.explanation: Amazon DynamoDB is a managed NoSQL database service.

questions.question: Which service can place messages between decoupled application components?
questions.explanation: Amazon SQS is a managed message queue service.

questions.question: Which service supports pub/sub messaging?
questions.explanation: Amazon SNS delivers messages to multiple subscribers.

questions.question: Which service helps process and analyze streaming data?
questions.explanation: Amazon Kinesis is used for streaming data ingestion and processing.

questions.question: Which service is designed for long-term archival with low retrieval frequency?
questions.explanation: S3 Glacier storage classes are intended for long-term, low-cost archival.

questions.question: Which AWS service provides a managed desktop experience in the cloud?
questions.explanation: Amazon WorkSpaces provides managed virtual desktops.

questions.question: Which AWS service can query data stored in S3 using SQL without managing servers?
questions.explanation: Amazon Athena queries data in S3 using serverless SQL.

questions.question: Which service is used to run containerized workloads?
questions.explanation: Amazon ECS is a managed container orchestration service.

questions.question: Which service provides a dedicated private network connection from on-premises to AWS?
questions.explanation: AWS Direct Connect provides dedicated private connectivity to AWS.

questions.question: Which service helps transfer large volumes of data physically into AWS?
questions.explanation: AWS Snowball is used for physical data transfer at scale.

questions.question: Which service provides in-memory caching for applications?
questions.explanation: Amazon ElastiCache provides managed in-memory caches.

questions.question: Which service helps build and orchestrate serverless workflows?
questions.explanation: Step Functions coordinates multiple serverless steps in a workflow.

questions.question: Which AWS service gives recommendations to right-size compute resources?
questions.explanation: Compute Optimizer analyzes usage patterns and suggests better sizing.

questions.question: Which service is specifically designed for data warehouse workloads?
questions.explanation: Amazon Redshift is a managed data warehouse service.

questions.question: Which service provides managed Kubernetes on AWS?
questions.explanation: Amazon EKS is AWS's managed Kubernetes service.

questions.question: Which service can automatically scale EC2 instances based on demand?
questions.explanation: EC2 Auto Scaling adjusts EC2 capacity based on policies and metrics.

questions.question: Which service helps monitor metrics, logs, and alarms across AWS resources?
questions.explanation: CloudWatch provides metrics, logs, dashboards, and alarms.

questions.question: Which service helps migrate databases to AWS with minimal downtime?
questions.explanation: AWS DMS helps move databases to AWS with minimal disruption.

questions.question: Which service lets customers run AWS infrastructure in their own data center?
questions.explanation: AWS Outposts extends AWS infrastructure and services on premises.

questions.question: Which service helps centrally manage operational tasks across many AWS resources?
questions.explanation: AWS Systems Manager helps manage operations at scale.

questions.question: Which AWS service helps manage encryption keys?
questions.explanation: AWS KMS creates and manages encryption keys.

questions.question: What is the main benefit of MFA on an AWS account?
questions.explanation: MFA adds another factor beyond a password.

questions.question: Which AWS service records API activity for auditing?
questions.explanation: CloudTrail records API calls and account activity.

questions.question: Which AWS service provides access to compliance reports and agreements?
questions.explanation: AWS Artifact provides compliance reports and agreements.

questions.question: Which IAM best practice reduces risk by limiting permissions?
questions.explanation: Least privilege grants only the permissions needed.

questions.question: Who is responsible for configuring IAM permissions in AWS?
questions.explanation: Customers are responsible for identity and access configuration.

questions.question: Which AWS service helps detect suspicious behavior and threats?
questions.explanation: GuardDuty analyzes activity to detect possible threats.

questions.question: Which concept means AWS secures the infrastructure while customers secure their data and configurations?
questions.explanation: The shared responsibility model splits duties between AWS and the customer.

questions.question: Which IAM identity should be avoided for everyday administrative tasks?
questions.explanation: The root user has full privileges and should be protected and used sparingly.

questions.question: Which AWS service evaluates AWS resource configurations for compliance rules?
questions.explanation: AWS Config tracks configuration changes and evaluates rules.

questions.question: Which security control can block unwanted inbound traffic to an EC2 instance?
questions.explanation: Security groups act as virtual firewalls for EC2 resources.

questions.question: Which AWS feature helps encrypt data stored in many AWS services?
questions.explanation: Many AWS services integrate directly with KMS for encryption at rest.

questions.question: Why should access keys be rotated periodically?
answers: To reduce risk if credentials are exposed
questions.explanation: Credential rotation limits exposure if old keys are compromised.

questions.question: Which AWS service helps inspect applications for software vulnerabilities on EC2 and containers?
questions.explanation: Amazon Inspector helps identify vulnerabilities and exposure.

questions.question: Which service helps protect web applications from common HTTP attacks?
questions.explanation: AWS WAF filters malicious web requests.

questions.question: Why are IAM roles preferred over embedding long-term credentials in code?
questions.explanation: IAM roles avoid hard-coded long-lived credentials by issuing temporary access.

questions.question: Which AWS tool helps identify opportunities to improve security posture and cost?
questions.explanation: Trusted Advisor provides checks across security, cost, and other categories.

questions.question: Which principle is most directly supported by separate AWS accounts for different environments?
questions.explanation: Separate accounts improve isolation and administrative boundaries.

questions.question: What is the primary reason to enable logging across AWS accounts centrally?
questions.explanation: Centralized logs improve visibility, monitoring, and investigation.

questions.question: Which approach best protects sensitive data if a storage bucket is misconfigured?
questions.explanation: Defense in depth uses encryption plus strict access control.

questions.question: Why is federated access useful in AWS?
answers: It lets users access AWS with external identities
questions.explanation: Federation allows trusted external identities to access AWS without separate native accounts for everyone.

questions.question: Which AWS service helps classify and protect sensitive data in S3?
questions.explanation: Macie helps discover and classify sensitive data in S3.

questions.question: Which control most directly supports nonrepudiation for AWS API activity?
questions.explanation: Retained audit logs help show who performed API actions and when.

questions.question: What is the best reason to avoid sharing AWS accounts across teams?
answers: It weakens accountability and access control
questions.explanation: Shared accounts reduce traceability and complicate least-privilege access.

questions.question: Which action most directly improves the security of the AWS root user?
answers: Enable MFA and avoid routine use
questions.explanation: The root user should be heavily protected and rarely used.

questions.question: Which AWS service helps protect against DDoS attacks at the network edge?
questions.explanation: AWS Shield helps defend against DDoS attacks.

questions.question: Which design improves security while reducing credential sprawl?
answers: Use roles and centralized identity management
questions.explanation: Roles and centralized identity improve control and reduce long-lived secrets.

questions.question: Why is encryption at rest useful in AWS?
answers: It helps protect stored data if underlying media is exposed
questions.explanation: Encryption at rest helps protect stored data from unauthorized disclosure.

questions.question: Which security objective is directly supported by AWS CloudTrail retention?
questions.explanation: Retained CloudTrail logs support governance, compliance, and investigations.

questions.question: Which action best reduces accidental over-permissioning in AWS?
answers: Review policies regularly and remove unused access
Use the root user more often
questions.explanation: Regular access reviews reduce unnecessary or outdated permissions.
